member.name: Lisa Smart
member.synopsis: Lisa Smart is the Liberal Democrat MP for Hazel Grove , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Cabinet Office).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Applications
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her department is taking to reduce the waiting times for visa administrative reviews.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Applications for administrative review have increased and intake has doubled within the last five years. Resource for the Administrative Review Unit is now more than double the equivalent 2021 staffing levels in response to this continuing high demand.Adm...

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Relations: Religion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he plans to take to support cross-community interfaith work.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Interfaith relations are vital for strong, cohesive communities. Learning about different faiths and beliefs builds mutual understanding and contributes to community cohesion. The government’s support for cross-community interfaith work is demonstrated by...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of not paying interest on unpaid compensation awards for the Infected Blood Compensation Scheme on claimants, the estates of deceased claimants and bereaved fa
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has decided not to adjust individual compensation awards to account for interest a person could have received, or investments that someone could have made or could make in the future. The compensation scheme does not pay interest on awards ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Russia: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether she has made an assessment of the level of the threat posed by Russia to UK elections.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to upholding and strengthening UK democracy by protecting against foreign interference, improving political transparency, adding tougher checks for donations and closing loopholes by reinforcing electoral legislation against fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Prescriptions: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent progress, he has made in reforming the Penalty Charge Notice system for unpaid prescription charges.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has no such plans for reforming the Penalty Charge Notice system.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability Aids: Recycling

writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Health Service is committed to reducing waste, whilst also delivering cost savings and minimising environmental impact, as set out in the Delivering a Net Zero NHS report. Since the publication of the report in 2020, there have been substanti...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Health: Medical Treatments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential of anti-USAG-1 antibody therapy for tooth regeneration; and what steps he is taking to ensure UK (a) researchers and (b) patients can partic
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds research through the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR), which supports clinical, public health, social care and global health research in partnership with the National Health Service, academia and wider stakeholde...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps were taken to help mitigate potential conflicts of interest arising from Lord Mandelson's commercial interests before she took up the post of Ambassador to the Uni
writtenQuestions.answerText: As has been made clear in documents that were published in the Government's response to the Humble Address, on 1 June 2026, measures were put in place to manage Peter Mandelson's declared conflicts of interest. As was also made clear in the Chief Secretar...

writtenQuestions.heading: Blue Badge Scheme

writtenQuestions.answerText: The model proforma was last reviewed in 2019 following the changes to the eligibility criteria. The Department works with the Blue Badge Digital Service supplier and with local authorities to help improve the service for all applications from people with ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Blue Badge Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness the Blue Badge application process for people with non-visible or hidden disabilities, including deafness.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The model proforma was last reviewed in 2019 following the changes to the eligibility criteria. The Department works with the Blue Badge Digital Service supplier and with local authorities to help improve the service for all applications from people with ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Prisoners
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of healthcare provision in the prison estate for prisoners with neuromuscular and other progressive degenerative conditions, including the ti
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England commissions prison health care services into every prison in England to the equivalence of the community, to include range, access and quality of service. Access to specialist consultants and referrals into specialised support is available in ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Probate: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that emergency applications for grants of probate are processed promptly; and whether he has made an assessment of the impact of staffing levels at the Probate Servic
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Courts & Tribunals Service has invested in more staff in 2026, alongside system and process improvements and a programme of upskilling in order to improve the processing time for applications and to ensure outstanding caseload does not increase. Se...

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability Living Allowance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if he will make an assessment of the potential impact of the (a) complexity and (b) length of the Disability Living Allowance claim form on claimants; and whether he plans to amend the form.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Disability Living Allowance claim form is under regular review to ensure that it is as easy to understand as possible, and that the information collected is complete. Recently, work has been undertaken to improve the legibility of the claim form. This...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that road safety interventions can be targeted at locations with high numbers of damage-only collisions and near-misses before a (a) killed and (b) seriously injure
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local authorities have a statutory duty under section 39 of the 1988 Road Traffic Act to take steps both to reduce and prevent collisions. It is not for the Department to take steps to define local interventions but for local authorities to determine what...

writtenQuestions.heading: Defibrillators
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help support shops, small businesses and public buildings to host publicly accessible defibrillators.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government’s position is that local communities are best placed to make decisions about procuring, locating, and maintaining automated external defibrillators (AEDs). Over 110,000 defibrillators are registered in the United Kingdom on The Circuit, the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The factors that determine whether a service is a prescribed specialised service are: the number of individuals who require the service; the cost of providing the service or facility; and the number of people able to provide the service or facility.Vacuum...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Disability Living Allowance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment his Department has made of the effectiveness of the Disability Living Allowance claim form; and whether he plans to take steps to make the form easier to complete.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
